orbitalTelescopeArray.name: Orbital Telescope Array
orbitalTelescopeArray.description: A constellation of imaging instruments placed in low orbit. Cheaper and faster than a full orbital platform, its primary value is signal: the array triangulates the transmission origin with an accuracy impossible from the ground. Requires a functioning Space Launch Centre.

hubbleSpaceTelescope.name: Orbital Imaging Platform
hubbleSpaceTelescope.description: A precision space telescope with spectroscopic and deep-field imaging capability. Above the atmosphere, the resolution improvements are transformative — anomalies in the outer solar system that appeared as statistical noise from the ground become geometric certainties. Requires Space Imaging technology and a Space Launch Centre.

governmentResearchContract.name: Government Research Contract
governmentResearchContract.description: The programme secures a multi-year public funding commitment in exchange for open publication of non-classified research findings. No upfront cost; yields a substantial funding injection over two turns.

technologyExportAgreement.name: Technology Export Agreement
technologyExportAgreement.description: Licences derived technologies to allied industrial partners. The materials investment covers packaging and certification; the return is substantial and sustained. Requires established orbital mechanics capability to have anything worth licensing.

orbitalStation_stage1.name: Core Module
orbitalStation_stage1.description: The pressurised core of the permanent orbital facility — life support, command systems, and docking collar. Once in orbit, it becomes humanity's first continuously-crewed outpost beyond the atmosphere.

orbitalStation_stage2.name: Habitation Ring
orbitalStation_stage2.description: The rotating habitation ring extends crew capacity from four to twelve and provides a stable platform for long-duration scientific work. Construction is complex: multiple launches, on-orbit assembly, and a narrow window for the final joining manoeuvre.

orbitalStation_stage3.name: Operational
orbitalStation_stage3.description: Final systems integration, deep-space antenna calibration, and crew rotation protocols are established. The Permanent Orbital Station is declared fully operational. The era of Near Space begins.

computingResearchProgramme.name: Computing Research Programme
computingResearchProgramme.description: Microprocessor clusters are reconfigured as dedicated scientific workstations — an order of magnitude faster than the mainframe batch-processing they replace. The Research Lab's existing experimental frameworks are rewritten to run on the new architecture; the payoff in computational research output is immediate and substantial.

cern.name: Particle Collider Complex
cern.description: A multi-kilometre underground ring accelerating particles to energies consistent with the mathematical framework in the signal's outer layers — not coincidentally. The outer structure of the transmission becomes legible at precision previously inaccessible. Expensive to construct and maintain, but the science yield is substantial and continuous.

lunarProspectingMission.name: Lunar Prospecting Mission
lunarProspectingMission.description: A systematic geological survey of candidate extraction sites across multiple lunar regions. Automated drills and chemical analysers map subsurface composition to depth — the data underpins every future extraction decision and reveals resource concentrations substantially richer than surface analysis suggested.

autonomousLunarSurveyGrid.name: Autonomous Lunar Survey Grid
autonomousLunarSurveyGrid.description: A fleet of autonomous robots systematically traverses the entire lunar near side, mapping subsurface structure, regolith depth, and resource concentrations in high resolution. The combined dataset is the most complete geological map of any extraterrestrial body in human history — and reveals that the near side is riddled with anomalies.

outerSystemProbe.name: Outer System Probe
outerSystemProbe.description: A nuclear-thermal probe launched on a trajectory toward the heliopause — the first human-made object designed to reach the boundary of the solar system. Its instruments are tuned to the signal's known frequency bands. Telemetry will take years to accumulate, but its launch is a statement of intent.

moonColony_stage1.name: Habitat Expansion
moonColony_stage1.description: Extending the initial lunar outpost with pressurised habitat modules and closed-loop life support. The first step toward a permanent human settlement beyond Earth orbit.

moonColony_stage2.name: Surface Infrastructure
moonColony_stage2.description: In-situ resource utilisation comes online: the colony now produces its own oxygen, water, and structural materials from lunar regolith. Supply costs for Lunar Surface facilities drop to zero.

moonColony_stage3.name: Self-Sustaining Colony
moonColony_stage3.description: The lunar colony is self-sustaining. For the first time in human history, a permanent settlement exists beyond Earth. The signal — sharper from the surface, stripped of ionospheric noise — is showing structure. Your analysts have begun to suspect it isn't transmitting to Earth at all.
